channel.title: Home on Jianchao Li
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Jianchao Li

item.title: The Double-Edged Sword of Compounding
item.description: While many investors understand the power of compound returns, they may not fully grasp that compounding can also result in a significant downside. This post explores this double-edged sword.

item.title: Interpret PyTorch Models with Captum
item.description: I used Captum to interpre the output of a MobileNetV2, which visualized the main regions in the input image that drove the model to generate its output.

item.title: From PyTorch to Core ML
item.description: I converted a PyTorch model to Core ML and ran it on an iPhone.

item.title: Connecting Computer Vision and Natural Language
item.description: I combined my previous posts on image captioning and visual question answering and extended them to a wider topic - connecting computer vision and natural language.

item.title: Killing Pytorch Multi Gpu Training the Safe Way
item.description: Recently I was working with PyTorch multi-GPU training and I came across a nightmare GPU memory problem. After some expensive trial and error, I finally found a solution for it.

item.title: Understanding Fully Convolutional Networks
item.description: I will start from the problem of semantic segmentation, introduce how to use CNNs to solve it, and talk about fully convolutional networks, a widely used framework for semantic segmentation, in great details. Moreover, I will analyze the MXNet implementation of FCNs.

item.title: A Dive Into Visual Question Answering
item.description: I read some papers on VQA and summarized its state-of-the-art, bottlenecks and possible solutions.

item.title: Playing With Image Captioning
item.description: I played with image captioning using neuraltalk2 written by Andrej Karpathy.

item.title: About
item.description:
Jianchao Li is a software engineer with expertise in deep learning, machine learning, and computer vision. He is currently based in Zurich, Switzerland and works at Meta. Prior to joining Meta, he has held software engineering roles at leading technology companies such as Indeed, ByteDance, and ViSenze.

Experience #

Software Engineer at Meta #

January 2021 - Present | Zurich, Switzerland
